Hey guys, welcome back to lesson 3 of module 1. I just want to take a quick second and introduce you to a term called engineering the conversion context and what this really is, is it's sequencing. So this is going to be a short lesson. I just want us to get very familiar with the terminology that we're going to be using from this point out. Now a lot of people always ask me how I sequence out these crazy long campaigns, you know, on the surface they see these 15, 20, 30, 40, sometimes even 50 email sequences and they freak out. They're like, I can never write something like that. But what they don't see is that I never actually sequence more than three or five emails in a row. Instead, there are these multiple sub narratives, each one with their own goal. So just like how a coach or a skilled therapist who is trying to facilitate a transformation for their client would need these parameters or boundaries to do it, you know, maybe several sessions each with their own theme or their own goal. The same really goes for our campaigns. So instead of one crazy long sequence, we're actually creating these multiple sub campaigns or conversion contexts that give the right focused attention to the beliefs and understandings that we're trying to instill or coach at that moment. So the big point here is that there is actually no such thing as a general product launch campaign. You kind of drop the anxiety around that, drop the anxiety around having to open up a Google doc and fill it with 30 or 40 new emails by the end of the week. Because there actually is no such thing. Instead there are these five core sub sequences that will appear across every funnel, every kind of launch campaign that you create. In the next lesson, you're actually going to get introduced to these five main sub sequences in further detail and in modules three through seven, you're going to get an even deeper dive into each of them, how they work, what we're coaching as well as the theme plates, the go to emails that I use to actually activate those principles or those beliefs or those attitudes that we are coaching. But for now, I just want you to take home the following. Number one, don't think of your campaigns as one super long sequence. Break them down into those sub sequences that specifically address the micro conversion that needs to take place to move your prospect along the buyer's journey. Then the last one is important. As our tools get more and more sophisticated, our CRMs, our email service providers, we're able to tap into so many different behavioral triggers and it becomes really tempting to play around with that and leverage all the things our tools could do. But I'm really going to ask you to just focus on these five core sub sequences that we're going to get to throughout this course and then get fancy later once you have those dialed in and to help you get fancy. Once you get there in the bonus section, you will learn a little concept I call contextology. That's how you can create more conversion context or profit minds as I like to call them within your funnel. These will be based on the behavioral triggers that your software flags down, whether people are opening, not opening, registering, not registering. These are the things that you're going to leverage through a concept I call contextology. It is a bit more advanced and it is something that goes beyond the scope of this training, but in that bonus, you are going to get the essentials, at least the skills to uncover those profit minds, as well as the few that I use in my own campaigns and campaigns for clients that really, really make the biggest impact on the bottom line. That's it for now. I want you to take a breath, soak this in, and when you're ready, come meet me in the next lesson where we're going to get a quicker overview of each of those five core sub sequences. I'll see you there.
